# App Store Mockup

> The simplest free App Store and Google Play mockup generator. Upload a screenshot, add a title, export. No signup, no watermark, runs entirely in your browser.

## What it does

App Store Mockup is a free, browser-based tool that generates professional App Store and Google Play screenshot mockups. Users upload their app screenshots, add titles and subtitles, choose from 3 beautiful layout variations (3D Angle, Top Device, Top Title), customize colors and fonts, and export high-resolution mockups ready for app store submission.

## Key features

- 3 professional mockup layouts: 3D Angle, Top Device, Top Title
- Multiple export resolutions: iPhone 6.7" (1290x2796), iPhone 6.5" (1242x2688), iPhone 6.1" (1179x2556), Google Play phone (1080x1920), iPad 12.9" (2048x2732), iPad 11" (1668x2388)
- Custom background colors and images
- Separate title and subtitle color pickers
- 9 Google Fonts with live preview: DM Sans, Inter, Poppins, Montserrat, Roboto, Raleway, Playfair Display, Lora, SF Pro
- Batch screenshot upload — generate mockups for multiple screens at once
- ZIP export with all selected variations and resolutions
- App title naming for exported ZIP files
- 100% client-side — images never leave the browser
- No signup, no account, no watermark
- Free forever

## Who it's for

- Indie app developers who need quick, professional App Store screenshots
- Mobile app designers creating store listing assets
- Startup teams preparing App Store or Google Play submissions
- Anyone who needs app mockups without learning Figma or Photoshop

## Comparison to alternatives

Unlike tools like Previewed, AppLaunchpad, or Screenshots Pro, App Store Mockup is:
- Completely free with no limits or watermarks
- Requires no account or signup
- Processes everything locally in the browser (privacy-first)
- Focuses on simplicity: 3 layouts, not 300 options

## URL

https://appstoremockup.com

## Tech stack

Single-page HTML/CSS/JS application. Uses html2canvas for rendering, JSZip for export bundling. No backend, no frameworks, no dependencies beyond those two libraries.
